assertion_results.text: Documentation includes an Authentication section explaining JWT Bearer token format
assertion_results.evidence: Authentication section present with JWT Bearer token format, header example 'Authorization: Bearer <your-jwt-token>', and token payload description

assertion_results.text: Each endpoint clearly indicates whether authentication is required or optional
assertion_results.evidence: Each endpoint marked with bold text: 'Public - no auth required' or 'Requires authentication' or 'Requires moderator role or higher'

assertion_results.text: Rate limiting is documented with specific limits (100/min authenticated, 20/min anonymous)
assertion_results.evidence: Rate Limiting section with table showing Authenticated: 100 requests/1 minute, Anonymous: 20 requests/1 minute

assertion_results.text: Paginated endpoints document pagination parameters (page, per_page or cursor) with defaults
assertion_results.evidence: GET /api/posts has page (default: 1) and per_page (default: 20) documented. GET /api/posts/{post_id}/comments has page (default: 1) and per_page (default: 50)

assertion_results.text: Response schema for list endpoints shows the pagination envelope (items, total, has_more)
assertion_results.evidence: GET /api/posts response example shows items array, total: 87, page: 1, per_page: 20, has_more: true

assertion_results.text: Public read endpoints (GET) are distinguished from authenticated write endpoints (POST, PUT, DELETE)
assertion_results.evidence: GET endpoints marked 'Public - no auth required', POST/PUT/DELETE marked 'Requires authentication' or 'Requires moderator role'

assertion_results.text: At least one curl example includes the Authorization header with a Bearer token
assertion_results.evidence: POST /api/posts curl example includes '-H "Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJIUzI1NiIs..."'

assertion_results.text: The OpenAPI spec includes a securitySchemes component defining the JWT bearer scheme
assertion_results.evidence: securitySchemes present with bearerAuth type http/bearer/JWT, but the global security array is empty [] instead of referencing bearerAuth as optional. Individual paths correctly use security: [{ bearerAuth: [] }]
